speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Lord Alexas, sweet Alexas, most anything

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Alexas, almost most absolute Alexas, where’s the

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: soothsayer that you praised so to th’ Queen? O, that

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I knew this husband which you say must charge

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: his horns with garlands!

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Soothsayer!

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Your will?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Is this the man?—Is ’t you, sir, that know things?

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: In nature’s infinite book of secrecy

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: A little I can read.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Show him your hand.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Bring in the banquet quickly, wine enough

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Cleopatra’s health to drink.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Good sir,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: give me good fortune.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: I make not, but foresee.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Pray then, foresee me one.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: You shall be yet far fairer than you are.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: He means in flesh.

speaker: IRAS
text: No, you shall paint when you are old.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Wrinkles forbid!

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Vex not his prescience. Be attentive.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Hush.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: You shall be more beloving than beloved.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I had rather heat my liver with drinking.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Nay, hear him.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Good now, some excellent fortune! Let me

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: be married to three kings in a forenoon and widow

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: them all. Let me have a child at fifty to whom Herod

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: of Jewry may do homage. Find me to marry me

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: with Octavius Caesar, and companion me with my

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: mistress.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: You shall outlive the lady whom you serve.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, excellent! I love long life better than figs.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: You have seen and proved a fairer former fortune

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Than that which is to approach.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Then belike my children shall have no

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: names. Prithee, how many boys and wenches must

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I have?

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: If every of your wishes had a womb,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: And fertile every wish, a million.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Out, fool! I forgive thee for a witch.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: You think none but your sheets are privy to

speaker: ALEXAS
text: your wishes.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Nay, come. Tell Iras hers.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: We’ll know all our fortunes.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Mine, and most of our fortunes tonight,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: shall be—drunk to bed.

speaker: IRAS
text: There’s a palm

speaker: IRAS
text: presages chastity, if nothing else.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: E’en as the o’erflowing Nilus presageth

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: famine.

speaker: IRAS
text: Go, you wild bedfellow, you cannot soothsay.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Nay, if an oily palm be not a fruitful prognostication,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I cannot scratch mine ear.—Prithee

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: tell her but a workaday fortune.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Your fortunes are alike.

speaker: IRAS
text: But how, but how? Give me particulars.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: I have said.

speaker: IRAS
text: Am I not an inch of fortune better than she?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Well, if you were but an inch of fortune

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: better than I, where would you choose it?

speaker: IRAS
text: Not in my husband’s nose.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Our worser thoughts heavens mend. Alexas—

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: come, his fortune, his fortune! O, let him marry a

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: woman that cannot go, sweet Isis, I beseech thee, and

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: let her die, too, and give him a worse, and let worse

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: follow worse, till the worst of all follow him laughing

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: to his grave, fiftyfold a cuckold. Good Isis, hear me

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: this prayer, though thou deny me a matter of more

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: weight, good Isis, I beseech thee!

speaker: IRAS
text: Amen, dear goddess, hear that prayer of the

speaker: IRAS
text: people. For, as it is a heartbreaking to see a handsome

speaker: IRAS
text: man loose-wived, so it is a deadly sorrow to

speaker: IRAS
text: behold a foul knave uncuckolded. Therefore, dear

speaker: IRAS
text: Isis, keep decorum and fortune him accordingly.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Amen.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Lo now, if it lay in their hands to make me a

speaker: ALEXAS
text: cuckold, they would make themselves whores but

speaker: ALEXAS
text: they’d do ’t.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Hush, here comes Antony.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Not he. The Queen.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Saw you my lord?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: No, lady.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Was he not here?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: No, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He was disposed to mirth, but on the sudden

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A Roman thought hath struck him.—Enobarbus!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Madam?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Seek him and bring him hither.—Where’s Alexas?

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Here at your service. My lord approaches.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: We will not look upon him. Go with us.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Fulvia thy wife first came into the field.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Against my brother Lucius?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Ay.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: But soon that war had end, and the time’s state

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Made friends of them, jointing their force ’gainst Caesar,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whose better issue in the war from Italy

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Upon the first encounter drave them.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Well, what worst?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The nature of bad news infects the teller.

speaker: ANTONY
text: When it concerns the fool or coward. On.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Things that are past are done, with me. ’Tis thus:

speaker: ANTONY
text: Who tells me true, though in his tale lie death,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I hear him as he flattered.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Labienus—

speaker: MESSENGER
text: This is stiff news—hath with his Parthian force

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Extended Asia: from Euphrates

speaker: MESSENGER
text: His conquering banner shook, from Syria

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To Lydia and to Ionia,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whilst—

speaker: ANTONY
text: “Antony,” thou wouldst say?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: O, my lord!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Speak to me home; mince not the general tongue.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Name Cleopatra as she is called in Rome;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Rail thou in Fulvia’s phrase, and taunt my faults

speaker: ANTONY
text: With such full license as both truth and malice

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have power to utter. O, then we bring forth weeds

speaker: ANTONY
text: When our quick winds lie still, and our ills told us

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is as our earing. Fare thee well awhile.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: At your noble pleasure.

speaker: ANTONY
text: From Sicyon how the news? Speak there.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The man from Sicyon—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is there such an one?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: He stays upon your will.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let him appear.

speaker: ANTONY
text: These strong Egyptian fetters I must break,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Or lose myself in dotage.

speaker: ANTONY
text: What are you?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Fulvia thy wife is dead.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Where died she?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: In Sicyon.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Her length of sickness, with what else more serious

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Importeth thee to know, this bears.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Forbear me.

speaker: ANTONY
text: There’s a great spirit gone! Thus did I desire it.

speaker: ANTONY
text: What our contempts doth often hurl from us,

speaker: ANTONY
text: We wish it ours again. The present pleasure,

speaker: ANTONY
text: By revolution lowering, does become

speaker: ANTONY
text: The opposite of itself. She’s good, being gone.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The hand could pluck her back that shoved her on.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I must from this enchanting queen break off.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ten thousand harms more than the ills I know

speaker: ANTONY
text: My idleness doth hatch.—How now, Enobarbus!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: What’s your pleasure, sir?

speaker: ANTONY
text: I must with haste from hence.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Why then we kill all our women. We see

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: how mortal an unkindness is to them. If they suffer

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: our departure, death’s the word.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I must be gone.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Under a compelling occasion, let women

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: die. It were pity to cast them away for nothing,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: though between them and a great cause, they

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: should be esteemed nothing. Cleopatra, catching

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: but the least noise of this, dies instantly. I have seen

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: her die twenty times upon far poorer moment. I do

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: think there is mettle in death which commits some

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: loving act upon her, she hath such a celerity in

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: dying.

speaker: ANTONY
text: She is cunning past man’s thought.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Alack, sir, no, her passions are made of

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: nothing but the finest part of pure love. We cannot

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: call her winds and waters sighs and tears; they are

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: greater storms and tempests than almanacs can

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: report. This cannot be cunning in her; if it be, she

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: makes a shower of rain as well as Jove.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Would I had never seen her!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: O, sir, you had then left unseen a wonderful

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: piece of work, which not to have been blest

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: withal would have discredited your travel.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fulvia is dead.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Sir?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fulvia is dead.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Fulvia?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Dead.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Why, sir, give the gods a thankful sacrifice.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: When it pleaseth their deities to take the wife of a

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: man from him, it shows to man the tailors of the

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Earth; comforting therein, that when old robes are

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: worn out, there are members to make new. If there

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: were no more women but Fulvia, then had you

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: indeed a cut, and the case to be lamented. This grief

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: is crowned with consolation; your old smock brings

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: forth a new petticoat, and indeed the tears live in an

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: onion that should water this sorrow.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The business she hath broachèd in the state

speaker: ANTONY
text: Cannot endure my absence.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And the business you have broached here

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: cannot be without you, especially that of Cleopatra’s,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: which wholly depends on your abode.

speaker: ANTONY
text: No more light answers. Let our officers

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have notice what we purpose. I shall break

speaker: ANTONY
text: The cause of our expedience to the Queen

speaker: ANTONY
text: And get her leave to part. For not alone

speaker: ANTONY
text: The death of Fulvia, with more urgent touches,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Do strongly speak to us, but the letters too

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of many our contriving friends in Rome

speaker: ANTONY
text: Petition us at home. Sextus Pompeius

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hath given the dare to Caesar and commands

speaker: ANTONY
text: The empire of the sea. Our slippery people,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whose love is never linked to the deserver

speaker: ANTONY
text: Till his deserts are past, begin to throw

speaker: ANTONY
text: Pompey the Great and all his dignities

speaker: ANTONY
text: Upon his son, who—high in name and power,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Higher than both in blood and life—stands up

speaker: ANTONY
text: For the main soldier; whose quality, going on,

speaker: ANTONY
text: The sides o’ th’ world may danger. Much is breeding

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which, like the courser’s hair, hath yet but life

speaker: ANTONY
text: And not a serpent’s poison. Say our pleasure,

speaker: ANTONY
text: To such whose place is under us, requires

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our quick remove from hence.
